calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Dec-30-2003 Vacated

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-30-2003 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled for hearing this day in Dept 218 with Commissioner Sue M. Kaplan, Clerk: V.Mack, Reporter: Patricia Van Steenberge CSR #1742
Appearances:
Nima Nami, Esq. present for and with Plaintiff Simon Leung. Chuck Lock, Cantonese translator for Plaintiff also present.
Gary Koon Chi Lam, Defendant in pro per present with Mandarin translator Luei Suen.
Parties met in Dept 504A with Judge Pro Tem Paul Arshawsky and reached a settlement by stipulation.
Terms of the ORAL settlement were read into the record as follows:
Defendant to vacate the premises by Jan. 30, 2004. Defendant waives return of security deposit and any interest on the security deposit.
If the defendant vacates the premises on or before Jan. 30, 2004, plaintiff waives all back rent, damages and will dismiss the action with prejudice.
Each party to bear its own costs and attorney's fees.
If defendant did not comply with the agreement, the stipulated judgment for possession will include a stipulation for back rent of $12,800.00 plus $60.00 a day damages beginning Sept. 16, 2003. Defendant agrees to no further damages to the premises and agrees to leave the premises in clean condition with no further waste or damages.
All parties agreed to the settlement in open court.
The Court deemed the matter settled and trial date is vacated.
Plaintiff's counsel to prepare this agreement in writing and submit to the court on Friday, Jan. 2, 2004.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-19-2003 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Gary Koon Chi Lam appearing in propria persona 359-5188, demurring party

RULING:
NO other APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING as amended: DEFENDANTs GARY Koon Chi LAM and SOO Chi LAM'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT -- Demurrer to First and Second C/A - Overruled; Demurrer to Fifth C/A Sustained with 5 days leave to amend as to what constitutes nuisance. If no amended complaint filed by plaintiff, then defendant has 10 days leave to answer. (301/JLW/JL-X) Reporter: Christina Paxton, CSR #1558
